desc: Kaunas is a compact Baltic city with interwar modernist architecture, riverfront walks, and a growing food-and-arts scene that feels less saturated than many capital-city breaks. Late spring to early autumn delivers the best weather and daylight, while winter can be excellent value for museums and short urban stays despite colder, cloudier conditions.
hls: Compact old town around Town Hall Square
Confluence of the Neris and Nemunas rivers
Strong museums and contemporary culture venues
Lower crowd pressure than many Baltic capitals
Good value in shoulder and winter periods

todo.name: Laisvės alėja + New Town modernism
todo.desc: Walk Kaunas's modernist core to see key 1920s-30s buildings and design landmarks.

todo.name: Old Town and Town Hall Square
todo.desc: Explore Kaunas Castle area, historic churches, and riverside streets in the old center.

todo.name: Nemunas & Neris confluence park
todo.desc: Visit Santaka Park for river views and easy walking routes near the old town.

todo.name: Čiurlionis National Art Museum
todo.desc: See Lithuania's major national art collections and works linked to M. K. Čiurlionis.

todo.name: Ninth Fort Museum
todo.desc: Plan a dedicated visit to this important 20th-century memorial and museum complex.

todo.name: Pažaislis Monastery and reservoir
todo.desc: Combine baroque monastery architecture with lakeside walking in the Kaunas outskirts.

wifi.note: Reliable in central accommodation and cafés; remote-work setup is usually straightforward.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; UK access is typically via Vilnius, Riga, or one-stop European routes. Typical return fares run around £60-£140 off-peak, £140-£220 in shoulder demand, and £220+ during peak summer and Christmas travel windows.
prac.visa: Lithuania is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Winters are cold and daylight is short; plan indoor attractions and layered clothing.
Peak summer weekends and festival dates increase central hotel prices and restaurant booking pressure.
Regional day trips are easier with a car outside core rail and bus corridors.

sweet: May-June and August-September are typically the strongest balance of long days, comfortable temperatures, and manageable demand.
